regions.name: Banks of the Superior Temporal Sulcus
Long Insular Gyrus and Central Insular Sulcus
Corpus Callosum
regions.description: The largest white matter commissure, connecting homologous regions across both cerebral hemispheres.
regions.fun_fact: The corpus callosum contains ~200 million axons — more than any other white matter tract.

regions.name: Anterior Commissure
regions.description: A small commissure crossing the midline, connecting the olfactory regions and anterior temporal lobes.
regions.fun_fact: The anterior commissure is phylogenetically ancient and present in all mammalian brains.

regions.name: Arcuate Fasciculus
regions.description: Connects Broca's area in the frontal lobe to Wernicke's area in the temporal lobe, forming the core language circuit.
regions.fun_fact: Damage to the arcuate fasciculus causes conduction aphasia — the inability to repeat words despite intact comprehension and production.

regions.name: Superior Longitudinal Fasciculus
regions.description: Connects parietal and frontal lobes, running along the dorsolateral convexity. Key for spatial attention and working memory.
regions.fun_fact: There are three distinct SLF branches (I, II, III) that connect different parietal and frontal regions.

regions.name: Inferior Fronto-Occipital Fasciculus
regions.description: A long tract running beneath the insula, connecting frontal lobe to occipital and temporal regions.
regions.fun_fact: The IFOF is thought to be involved in semantic processing and visual attention.

regions.name: Inferior Longitudinal Fasciculus
regions.description: Connects temporal pole to occipital cortex along the ventral stream, supporting visual recognition and memory.
regions.fun_fact: The ILF is a key tract for face recognition — damage causes prosopagnosia.

regions.name: Uncinate Fasciculus
regions.description: Hooks around the Sylvian fissure, connecting orbitofrontal cortex to anterior temporal lobe.
regions.fun_fact: The uncinate fasciculus is one of the last tracts to mature — development continues into the third decade of life.

regions.name: Vertical Occipital Fasciculus
regions.description: A short vertical tract in the posterior brain connecting dorsal and ventral occipital cortex.
regions.fun_fact: First described by Wernicke in 1881, the VOF was largely forgotten for over a century before being rediscovered by diffusion MRI.

regions.name: Corticospinal Tract
regions.description: Descends from motor cortex through the internal capsule and brainstem to the spinal cord, carrying voluntary motor commands.
regions.fun_fact: The CST decussates (crosses) in the medullary pyramids — the left motor cortex controls the right side of the body.

regions.name: Optic Radiation
regions.description: Carries visual signals from the lateral geniculate nucleus of the thalamus to primary visual cortex.
regions.fun_fact: Meyer's loop — the anterior bend of the optic radiation — sweeps through the temporal lobe. Temporal lobe surgery can cause a 'pie-in-the-sky' visual field defect.
